summary:
Adjusting the cuspal inclinations by grinding them correctly—allowing for lateral sliding—is necessary to prevent edge-to-edge contact during centric occlusion, promoting comfortable and functional dental prostheses. This involves making sure the upper cusp tilts towards the cheek side as it contacts against its counterpart from below which moves toward the tongue side. Thus, option C represents an appropriate solution. \(\boxed{\text{Grinding}}\) Summary:

To resolve edge-to-edge contact in complete dentures during centric occlusion, modify the cuspal inclines through careful grinding; this facilitates smoother jaw movements and prevents undue stress or wear. Specifically, ensure the upper cusp slopes buccally when contacting the lower cusp moving lingually. \(\boxed{\text{Adjustment via grinding}}\).

Specifically, ensure the upper cusp slopes buccally when contacting the lower cusp moving lingually. \(\boxed{\text{Adjustment via grinding}}\).. Answer choice: C
